search_queries_used: site:asahq.org perioperative obstructive sleep apnea preoperative evaluation airway examination
site:rcoa.ac.uk GPAS obstructive sleep apnea anesthesia review
site:doh.gov.ae day surgery sleep apnea anesthesia
candidate_sources_rejected: automatic management or dose instructions for Obstructive sleep apnea anesthesia review
generic anesthesia landing pages without an exact section for Obstructive sleep apnea anesthesia review
rejection_reasons: Scores and disposition require validated inputs and clinician interpretation and were not mapped.
The candidate source category "automatic postoperative disposition rules" did not provide exact documentation-only evidence for this workflow and was not mapped.
Autonomous management, medication, dose, procedure, escalation, or discharge instructions were outside the documentation-only scope.
Landing pages and search snippets were not accepted as exact evidence.
population_applicability: Adults and children older than 1 year with suspected or established OSA in the ASA source.
setting_applicability: Direct for preoperative anesthesia review; diagnosis, treatment, and postoperative location remain clinician-led.
UAE_applicability: International ASA evidence requires UAE sleep, anesthesia, and facility pathway adaptation.
recency_verification: Official ASA guideline published 2014 plus current ASA 2023 documentation and RCoA GPAS 2025 were reviewed on 2026-07-13; ASA recency is a blocker.
superseded_check: No newer full ASA OSA perioperative guideline was identified.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ASA section supports record review, patient or family interview, airway examination, previous airway difficulty, OSA symptoms, sleep-study results, and severity documentation without inferring OSA or management.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports record review, patient or family interview, airway examination, prior airway difficulty, OSA symptoms, sleep-study results, and severity documentation.

evidence_items.direct_relationship: The exact ASA preanesthesia documentation section supports risk, airway, cardiorespiratory, reviewed-investigation, consultation, plan, and consent fields for Obstructive sleep apnea anesthesia review; each remains clinician-entered and conditional on assessment.
evidence_items.paraphrased_evidence_summary: Supports identity, disposition, medical, surgical and anesthetic history, medicines, allergies and reactions, fasting, advance directives, airway and cardiopulmonary examination, tests, consultations, ASA status, plan including postanesthesia and pain care, and consent.

unresolved_source_gaps: Screening score, OSA diagnosis, sleep-study interpretation, device use, severity conclusion, opioid plan, monitoring frequency, admission, and discharge remain unsupported.
Legacy duration presets, broad generic interval-history wording, and any content not explicitly mapped to an exact section remain unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No source mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, anesthesia plan, investigation, procedure, medication, dose, referral, discharge decision, or urgency conclusion.
UAE facility policy, credentialing, pathway, and qualified anesthesiologist review remain required before use.
